emails.subject: Re: revised language
emails.body: Good point, but I'm comfortable we're covered because our bidders were all bidding on a chunk of capacity and primary points. They are deemed to be on notice that they had alternate point rights (its in the tariff) and if they wanted to submit a bid that had a different rate for primaries and alternates, they could have done so. None of them did, and most importantly, the winning bidders did not. Our letter agreement simply memorializes that we will charge the same negotiated rate whether the gas flows on primaries or alternates. I assume from your message you are OK with this and they can get it nailed down? DF From: Mary Kay Miller 01/11/2001 02:49 PM

emails.subject: Re: revised language
emails.body: If everyone else is ok, I can be however, I believe the tariff does not grant these alternate point rights and by just adding we could be sitting a precedent. especially in regard to outside the path. MK Drew Fossum 01/11/2001 02:59 PM
